title: If I Were You – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who is Gerrard?
questions.answer: Gerrard is a playwright who lives alone in a cottage.

questions.question: Who enters Gerrard’s cottage?
questions.answer: A criminal intruder enters Gerrard’s cottage.

questions.question: Why did the intruder enter Gerrard’s cottage?
questions.answer: The intruder entered to kill Gerrard and take his identity.

questions.question: What weapon did the intruder carry?
questions.answer: The intruder carried a revolver.

questions.question: Why was the intruder running from the police?
questions.answer: He was running from the police because he had killed a policeman.

questions.question: How did Gerrard behave when the intruder threatened him?
questions.answer: Gerrard remained calm and spoke confidently.

questions.question: What did the intruder want from Gerrard?
questions.answer: He wanted to kill Gerrard and live under his identity.

questions.question: What trick did Gerrard use to confuse the intruder?
questions.answer: Gerrard pretended that he himself was a criminal.

questions.question: Why did Gerrard pretend to be a criminal?
questions.answer: He did it to confuse the intruder and gain time.

questions.question: What quality helped Gerrard save himself?
questions.answer: His intelligence and presence of mind helped him.

questions.question: Where did Gerrard trap the intruder?
questions.answer: He trapped the intruder in a cupboard.

questions.question: What happened to the intruder’s revolver?
questions.answer: The revolver fell from the intruder’s hand.

questions.question: What did Gerrard do after trapping the intruder?
questions.answer: He picked up the revolver and called the police.

questions.question: Why did the intruder want Gerrard’s identity?
questions.answer: He believed it would help him escape the police.

questions.question: What kind of atmosphere does the play create?
questions.answer: The play creates a tense and suspenseful atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the main conflict in the play?
questions.answer: The intruder threatens Gerrard’s life to take his identity.

questions.question: How does Gerrard control the situation?
questions.answer: He controls the situation through clever conversation.

questions.question: What mistake did the intruder make?
questions.answer: He trusted Gerrard’s false story.

questions.question: Why is Gerrard considered clever?
questions.answer: Because he defeats the intruder without violence.

questions.question: What literary device is used in Gerrard’s speech?
questions.answer: Irony is often used in his speech.

questions.question: What message does the play convey?
questions.answer: The play conveys that intelligence is stronger than violence.

questions.question: What does the title 'If I Were You' suggest?
questions.answer: It suggests giving advice or warning to someone.

questions.question: How does the play end?
questions.answer: The intruder is trapped and the police are called.

questions.question: What role does dialogue play in the story?
questions.answer: Dialogue helps develop the plot and reveal characters.

questions.question: What lesson can readers learn from Gerrard’s actions?
questions.answer: They learn that calm thinking can overcome danger.
